review_text:
Campo San Bortolomio is a charming and bustling square in Venice, perfectly situated near the iconic Rialto Bridge. Surrounded by elegant boutiques and high-end shops, it is a lively meeting point where both locals and visitors gather to soak in the city’s vibrant atmosphere. The square’s energy is enhanced by its rich history and architectural beauty, making it an essential stop for anyone exploring Venice.

Fittingly, amidst the luxury and grandeur, you will find a statue dedicated to none other than Giacomo Casanova, the infamous Venetian adventurer and seducer. His presence in this sophisticated setting feels almost poetic—after all, Casanova was known for his love of fine things, from fashion to literature. The statue serves as a reminder of Venice’s alluring past, where romance, intrigue, and elegance intertwined seamlessly.

Whether you’re pausing to admire the statue, indulging in some high-end shopping, or simply enjoying the lively atmosphere, Campo San Bortolomio offers a perfect blend of history and modern charm. Its location near the Rialto ensures it remains a popular spot, yet it still retains a unique character that makes it worth more than just a passing glance.

shop_address: 30124 Venezia VE, Italia
review_text: During the walk from St. Mark's Square to the Academy Bridge, we pass this beautiful square. This elongated square is one of the most elegant in Venice. Campo Santo Stefano or Campo Morosini is one of the largest fields in Venice. It is surrounded by beautiful churches and palaces such as the church of Santo Stefano, the church of San Vidal, Palazzo Morosini, Palazzo Loredan and Palazzo Pisani and the adjacent Campiello Pisani. It is a favorite gathering place of locals and guests, sometimes concerts, Christmas and other fairs are held on this square. And it is certainly very interesting to walk here during the Carnival. In the center of the field stands the statue of the writer Niccolò Tommaseo, the work of Francesco Barzaghi from 1882. The pleasant atmosphere is complemented by coffee bars, patisseries and restaurants such as "Ristorante Pizzeria Santo Stefano", Ristorante "Le Café", Ristorante " A Beccafico ", Ristorante / Bar" Art Blue Café ", Osteria" Ethnos ", Osteria" Doge Morosini ", Gelateria" Paolin "and on the corner and opposite the church the popular Bar" all'Angolo ". Right behind the “Art Blue Cafe” and Palazzo Morosini Gatterburg is the far-famed Venetian Music Conservatory of Music Benedetto Marcello. Every time we pass there we hear good music spreading towards the square from the building. It is interesting and good to know that the Church of San Vidal is a place where classical music concerts of the famous Rondo Veneziano are held. The pride of the church is its very fine Gothic portal, created in 1442 by Bartolomeo Bon. The interior is decorated with ingenious works by Tintoretto.

review_text: Il Campo de la Maddalena è una piazza situata nel sestiere di Cannaregio a Venezia. È caratterizzata dalla presenza della Chiesa della Maddalena, che le dà il nome. La chiesa, dedicata a Maria Maddalena, risale al XII secolo, ma è stata ricostruita nel XVIII secolo in stile barocco. Il campo è un luogo incantevole, con il suo caratteristico campanile e la presenza di alcuni caffè e ristoranti che lo rendono un luogo piacevole per rilassarsi e ammirare l'atmosfera veneziana.

shop_address: Campo Santi Giovanni e Paolo, 30122, VE, Italia
review_text: After Piazza San Marco, the Campo Santi Giovanni e Paolo was considered by the Venetians, for various reasons, the most important and famous field in the city. First of all, because in this place solemn feasts and ceremonies were often organized and then because the bodies of the Doges and high representatives of the Republic passed through this field before receiving the last anointing in the adjacent Basilica of Santi Giovanni e Paolo, called in Venetian San Zanipolo. In the center of the field there is a beautiful wellhead from 1500, decorated with cherubs holding fruit festoons. Near the wellhead we  admired the equestrian monument of Bartolomeo Colleoni, a beautiful bronze sculpture made between 1481 and 1488 almost certainly by the Florentine artist Andrea Verocchio (1400), master of Leonardo da Vinci (1400-1500). Bartolomeo Colleoni was an important leader from Bergamo, who worked in the service of the Republic of Venice. Campo Santi Giovanni e Paolo hosts an extremely beautiful building, and it is the Scuola Grande di San Marco. As soon as you see it you will be delighted. The Scuola Grande di San Marco is one of the six Scuole Grandi in Venice, and the real masterpice of architecture. Its asymmetrical facade built of white marble, with niches and statues and a mixture of Renaissance and Byzantine style of construction confirms that it is a real gem.

shop_address: C. del Tentor, 1602, 30135 Venezia VE, Italia
review_text:
Campo San Giacomo dell'Orio is a lovely and serene piazza located in Venice's Santa Croce area. This plaza, known for its quiet mood, rich foliage, and closeness to the titular church, provides a peaceful reprieve from the busy throng of more popular sites such as San Marco.

The square's name comes from the Chiesa di San Giacomo dall'Orio, a historic church on its eastern side. The church has a long history, dating back to the ninth century, with the oldest written reference coming from 1089. The word "Orio" is said to allude to the laurel trees that previously grew in the region.

Campo San Giacomo dell'Orio is a wonderful spot to rest and unwind, owing to its tranquil setting and leafy trees. It's the ideal place to escape the rush and bustle of Venice.

The Church of San Giacomo dell'Orio: This historic church is one of Venice's oldest, with amazing architecture and artwork. While the façade is very modest, the interior is filled with lovely paintings and furnishings.
Campo San Giacomo dell'Orio serves as a gathering area for residents. You may immerse yourself in Venetian culture by having a coffee or eating at one of the nearby cafés or restaurants.

A historic well in the heart of the Piazza serves as a reminder of Venice's history.

This piazza gives a peaceful respite from Venice's tourist-heavy districts, giving a more authentic and local experience.

The Chiesa di San Giacomo dall'Orio, with its rich history and distinctive architecture, is well worth a visit.

Sitting in a café and watching daily life develop is a unique way to explore Venetian culture.

shop_address: C. del Tentor, 1602, 30135 Venezia VE, Italia
review_text: If you're looking to experience Venice beyond its tourist hotspots and want to soak in the local culture, Campo San Giacomo dell'Orio is a must-visit destination. It provides a glimpse into the everyday life of Venetians and offers a refreshing change of pace from the crowded tourist areas. Whether you're interested in people-watching, enjoying a drink, or simply savoring the lively ambiance, this square is sure to leave a lasting impression.

review_text:
Campo San Silvestro is a charming square located in the heart of Venice, exuding a quintessentially Venetian atmosphere. Surrounded by quaint buildings and lively cafes, it serves as a gathering place for both locals and visitors. The square is named after the nearby church of San Silvestro, adding to its historical appeal.

One of the highlights of Campo San Silvestro is the vibrant energy that fills the air, especially during the day when the sun casts a warm glow over the area. It’s an ideal spot to take a leisurely stroll, enjoy a coffee while people-watching, or simply soak in the ambiance of Venice.

While it may not be as famous as some of the larger piazzas, Campo San Silvestro offers a more intimate experience, showcasing the charm and character of the city. Overall, it's a delightful stop that encapsulates the essence of Venice’s unique blend of history and everyday life.

review_text:
Piacevole campo veneziano Sestiere San Polo , ecco la storia:

In questo campo, dove abito' Aldo Manuzio il celebre tipografo del 1500, era collocata una colonna d'infamia, che indicava originariamente il luogo dove si trovava la casa di Bajamonte Tiepolo che nel 1310, assieme a Querini e Badoer, architetto' la celebre congiura in seguito alla serrata del maggior consiglio. Dopo la sconfitta di Bajamonte e dei duoi complici, la sua casa venne abbattuta ed al suo posto fu collocata nel 1364 una colonna d'infamia, che recava la seguente iscrizione: 'DE BAJAMONTE FO QUESTO TERENO E MO PER LO SO INIQUO TRADIMENTO S'e' POSTO IN CHOMUN PER ALTRUI SPAVENTO E PER MOSTRAR A TUTI SEMPRE SENO'. ( Questo terreno fu di Bajamonte Tiepolo ed ora per il suo iniquo tradimento e' diventato pubblico e queste parole siano sempre mostrate a tutti per monito agli altri ). Poco dopo il suo innalzamento la colonna venne danneggiata da un certo Francesco Fantebon che era stato un complice di Bajamonte Tiepolo e per questo condannato e poi anche graziato. A seguito del suo gesto di spregio, il Fontebon venne punito con il taglio di una mano, la perdita degli occhi e l'esilio. Anche il Tiepolo aveva il suo palazzo qui.

shop_address: 3360, 30121 Venezia VE, Italia
review_text: O Campo dei Mori é uma praça no bairro de Cannaregio , em Veneza . Seu nome vem de três estátuas de mármore, uma quarta fica ao lado da Fondamenta dei Mori, representando orientais com turbantes (adicionados posteriormente). Eles são datados do século XIII/XIV. Muitas vezes acredita-se que as três estátuas do Campo representam três irmãos gregos chamados Rioba, Sandi e Alfani, membros da família Mastelli, que se mudaram para Veneza como comerciantes no século XII. Eles foram chamados de "Mori" porque vieram da Moreia .

La plaza o campo toma su nombre de las estatua de los 3 moros, que corresponde a una familia de ricos comerciantes conocidos como los hermanos Mastelli, sobre estas estatuas se ha desarrollado una leyenda, la cual es curiosa, como recuerdo de la visitas no llevamos las fotos.

shop_address: 3360, 30121 Venezia VE, Italia
review_text:
Il campo prende nome dalle statue dei 3 mori (mori intesi come provenienti dalla Morea, attuale Peloponneso).
I 3 mori sono i fratelli Mastelli, ricchi mercanti che avevano casa (vedi palazzo Mastelli del cammello) e magazzino (vedi casa del Tintoretto) proprio in queste fondamenta (chiamate giustappunto Fondamenta dei mori).
Le statue rappresentano la pietrificazione dei 3 fratelli ad opera della Madonna, invocata da un'anziana devota, raggirata dai 3 abili mercanti truffaldini e quindi poste nel campo come eterno promemoria per i commercianti con cattive intenzioni.
La quarta statua è posizionata sulla casa del Tintoretto, ex fondaco (magazzino) dei fratelli Mastelli, e rappresenta il loro fedele servitore.

shop_address: 3360, 30121 Venezia VE, Italia

Wir haben den Campo eher zufällig entdeckt. Er liegt abseits der Hauptrouten und ist deshalb relativ ruhig. Benannt ist er nach den Statuen an den Hauswänden, den Mori. Sie sollen drei versteinerte Brüder und ihren Diener darstellen.
Die Sage erzählt, dass die Brüder zur Strafe betrogen wurden und der arme Diener gleich mit.
Tipp: "111 Orte in Venedig, die man gesehen haben muss" von Anna Toscano und Gianni Montieri (Emons 2023). Dort kann man die Sage nachlesen.

shop_address: S. Polo, 30100 Venezia VE, Italia
review_text:
Campo San Polo, Venice's second biggest piazza after Saint Mark's Square, is a vast and attractive sanctuary located in the center of the San Polo neighborhood. This rectangular space, spanning 100 meters by 60 meters, provides a pleasant relief from the city's winding lanes.

Originally utilized as pasture and farming, the plaza was entirely paved in 1493 and ornamented with one of Venice's few wells. Throughout history, it has hosted a variety of events, including bullfights, religious speeches, and elaborate masked parties. In the seventeenth century, it was also used as a marketplace.

Today, Campo San Polo is a popular gathering spot for both locals and visitors. Venetian families frequently gather here to enjoy the open area. During the Venice Film Festival, the plaza holds open-air music and films. It also serves as a prominent location for the Venice Carnival.

If you're ready to sacrifice some sleep, an early morning visit will reward you with the entire piazza to yourself. I took full advantage of this quiet opportunity to take some breathtaking images.

shop_address: S. Polo, 30100 Venezia VE, Italia
review_text: This is the second largest square in Venice. Originally used as pastureland for crops and livestock, it was fully paved in 1493, with a central well added soon after. Until the 17th century, it served as a venue for local markets and a wide range of public events—including bullfights, masquerade balls, religious gatherings, and even dogfighting. Today, the square hosts major Carnival events, outdoor concerts, and film screenings during the Venice Film Festival.

Venedik'te San Marco'dan sonraki en geniş meydan olma özelliğine sahip olan San Polo,  daha öncesinde mera iken 1493 yılında ortasında da bir kuyu (çeşme) yerleştirilerek boğa güreşleri, toplu ibadetler ve maskeli balolar gibi birbirinden farklı kalabalık etkinliklerin düzenlenmesine ev sahipliği yapmış. Günümüzde de festivallere ev sahipliği yapan bir alan olma özelliğini sürdürüyor.

San Polo Kilisesi, Tiepolo Sarayı, Soranzo Sarayı, Dona Brusa Sarayı Meydanda bulunan önemli tarihi yapıların başlıcalarıdır.

review_text: Чудесная церковь 14 века, построенная в готическом стиле. Ее возвели умилиаты (орден смиренников) и посвятили святому Христофору, покровителю путешественников и корабельщиков. Она двуцветная - красный кирпич и белый известняк декоративных деталей. А в 1377 году церковь стала называться Мадонна-дель-Орто (Мадонна в саду), в честь чудотворной скульптуры сидящей Мадонны с Младенцем работы Джованни Де Санти. Статуя стояла в саду скульптора и вскоре прохожие стали замечать исходящий от нее свет, появились паломники, начались исцеления. Эта новость разлетелась по всему городу, и скульптора начали уговаривать перенести Мадонну в церковь. Так в незаконченном виде она за 150 дукатов переехала на новое место. Сейчас статую можно увидеть в правой капелле от алтаря. Церковь Мадонны в саду нераздельно связана с именем Якопо Робусти, прозванного Тинторетто, который жил неподалёку и был прихожанином этой церкви. По заказу общины он написал несколько картин, в том числе  «Поклонение золотому тельцу», «Чудо св. Агнессы», «Введение во храм», «Страшный суд». Есть в храме и работы его сына Доменико «Рождество с Ангелами». Сейчас эти шедевры украшают стены церкви. Тинторетто был человеком глубоко верующим и был похоронен в церкви в 1594 году. Вблизи алтаря, в боковом приделе справа, находится его могильная плита. Здесь же в капелле с правой стороны похоронены его дети, оба художники - сын Доменико и дочь Мариетта. В 1669 году  церковь была передана цистерцианцам Ломбардии, а в 1787 - перешла в государственное управление. В капеллах боковых нефов можно увидеть картины многих выдающихся живописцев. Некогда в капелле Валье над алтарём находилась картина Джованни Беллини  «Мадонна с Младенцем» (1481). Она была украдена из церкви в 1993 году и до сих пор не найдена. Так и осталась пустовать рама картины. Вход в церковь платный 3 евро. Время посещения - февраль 2023.

review_text: hotel was excellent location. 7 minute easy walk from water taxi. gondola station right outside the hotel & located amongst all the shops / restaurants etc.|Rooms are basic but nice & clean & safe & in Venice your not in your room much.|breakfast included, very very basic but fills a little hole & for the price in that location, you really can't complain. there's a cafe right next door if you wanted more breakfast.|excellent mini bar in room but has to be paid for if used, fair enough.|room was spacious. extra thick blankets in wardrobe if needed.|shampoo, soap, shower gel, toilet roll, hair dryer all provided in room.|would 100% stay here again & would recommend  it. overall it was perfect.|No kettle / tea / coffee making facilties but we asked for a kettle & got one straight away no problem.|No water in room but there's a convenience store right next door so not a problem.

shop_address: Calle al Ponte de la Guerra, 511, 30124 Venezia VE, Italia
review_text:
We had a wonderful stay at Hotel Ca' Del Campo with our family of four for two nights. Our room was spacious, and we were delighted to have a view of the canal right from our window. The location is fantastic, just a 5-minute walk to San Marco's, and there are plenty of excellent restaurants around.

The staff were incredibly friendly and helpful throughout our stay. We had an early flight in the morning, and they assisted us in confirming a water taxi to pick us up at 4:00 AM. They even walked with us to the deck and helped us lift our luggage onto the boat. To top it off, the staff sent us off with a bag of snacks and water since we didn't have time for breakfast.

Overall, our experience at Hotel Ca' Del Campo was exceptional, and we highly recommend it to anyone visiting the area.

Close to San Marco Square once you find your way (or google maps is your friend).

review_text:
Schöner und ruhiger Innenhof. Keine besondere Architektur. Was aber den Ort so interessant macht, ist die Geschichte. Daher unbedingt den Audio Guide nutzen. Man erfährt viel über die Geschichte des Platzes, die Synagogen, das Judentum und die Gegenwart.
Leider konnte man von 5 Synagogen nur 2 betreten, die andern waren wegen Umbau- und Renovierungsarbeiten gesperrt. Ebenso die Bäckerei. Trotzdem hat es sich für mich gelohnt, da ich davor nur wenig über die Rituale und Besonderheiten des jüdischen Lebens wusste.

shop_address: Cannaregio, 2873c, 30121 Venezia VE, Italia
review_text:
Il Ghetto Nuovo si trova nel sestiere di Cannaregio a Venezia e si presenta tuttora come un'isola, i cui accessi avvengono solo tramite ponti. L’omonimo campo occupa gran parte della zona centrale. Fanno da cornice al campo la casa di riposo israelitica, alcuni locali kosher, il museo ebraico, le sinagoghe tedesca e italiana oltre agli alti edifici con abitazioni private. Nel campo ci sono tre vere da pozzo quattrocentesche due in marmo rosa di Verona ed una in pietra d’Istria.
Assolutamente da vedere!!!

Calle de la Mandola, 3654/A, 30124 Venezia VE, Italia
review_text:
Campo Marzio Roma 1933 is a hidden gem for those who appreciate fine craftsmanship. This charming boutique specializes in exquisite fountain pens and high-quality leather goods, blending Italian elegance with affordability. We first discovered it thanks to our friends, who stumbled upon it during a shopping spree. They were so impressed by the beautifully crafted passport holders that they insisted we visit—and we’re glad they did.

The selection of leather goods was stunning, with rich textures and impeccable detailing. The passport holders, in particular, stood out for their elegance and durability, and at such a reasonable price, they felt like an absolute steal. The gentleman assisting us was incredibly knowledgeable and took the time to explain the difference between cheap, mass-produced leather and the premium leather used in their products. It was a fascinating insight that deepened our appreciation for the craftsmanship.

Since purchasing mine, I’ve received countless compliments whenever I travel. It’s not just a functional piece—it’s a statement of style and quality. If you ever find yourself in Rome, Campo Marzio Roma 1933 is a must-visit for anyone who loves timeless accessories that blend beauty with practicality.

Ci sono stato per un concerto di un gruppo di musica folk, organizzato da un gruppo di Balfolk. Un'iniziativa bella, divertente e forse anche un po' atipica per una parrocchia che, credo, dimostra l'apertura mentale del parroco e di chi gestisce lo spazio.

shop_address: Fondamenta Sant'Elena, 5, 30132 Venezia VE, Italia
review_text: This is what football is all about! Such a cool environment, very local, very warm and including fans, and a very historic stadium, since last year I wanted to go and there was no game that weekend, now I came back to visit Venice and of course I had to go for a Venezia FC game! The beautiful game! Grazie mille per tutti raga veneziana :)

shop_address: Fondamenta Sant'Elena, 5, 30132 Venezia VE, Italia
review_text: Good atmosphere, but there has to be more seats for a Serie A team. Relegated though.

shop_address: Fondamenta Sant'Elena, 5, 30132 Venezia VE, Italia
review_text: Me and my friends had the day in Venice and decided to try and walk to the Pier Luigi Penzo Stadium without using our location on our phones. After many hours we managed to get to the stadium and it didn't disappoint. Its a very unique stadium and I was very pleased with the look. There was a game on but sadly it was behind closed doors so I was able to get into the stadium. Hopefully one day I can return.

shop_address: Fondamenta Sant'Elena, 5, 30132 Venezia VE, Italia
review_text: Amazing place for a groundhopping experience. The stadium definitely had better times back in the days, but I liked its charm and character. The local team, Venice FC, plays in Seria B - the second Italian division. Entrance fees start from 24€ (respectively 12€ for people under 30 and above 65 years) which is quite high in my opinion. Even tough I really enjoyed the experience, beer prices are a huge rip-off. 6,5€ for 0,5l out of a plastic cup is just too much!

shop_address: Fondamenta Sant'Elena, 5, 30132 Venezia VE, Italia
review_text:
Unique stadium due to its location and arrival to it is by water, however the stadium is very basic. Three uncovered stands, three of which look as if they're constructed from meccano, with a 'main stand' that is covered however has pillars obstructing views.

Limited facilities, however my Wife said the ladies toilets are better than those at San Siro. Food kiosks sold beer, coffee,, water and soft drinks with limited food options. Beer at €5 was cheapest we had anywhere in Venice.

No program, no souvenir or fan shop or stalls by ultras or other vendors.

Ticket in curva only €12 so very cheap. Vaporetto stop short walk through pleasant green space and residential area. Not much around really. Found a little tratorria pre match to get out the rain and have a beer.

Worth a visit if you're a football or stadium hopper, however is nt the 'can't miss' yiu might have thought.

shop_address: Fondamenta Sant'Elena, 5, 30132 Venezia VE, Italia
review_text: Great location. Small and welcoming. Food and beer is priced well unlike at English football games. Very Sunny in the Curva and Distinti, cold but rainproof in the Tributa Verde

shop_address: Fondamenta Sant'Elena, 5, 30132 Venezia VE, Italia
review_text: Disappointed to find the grounds were locked today.  Hoping to get to see the pitch and maybe get to a team merchandise store and buy some stuff. From what we saw, looks very cool and old school.   I imagine watching a game here is amazing

review_text: Few random unsorted shots of tonight’s Coppa Italia 2nd round game between Venezia and Carrarese. 1000 people allowed in with social distancing to Italian games now, booked in advance. Venice won 2-0. Standard no better than national league IMHO but interestingly Carrarese are part owned by Italian GK legend Buffon who comes from there I believe.

review_text: Piacevole visita. Ma l'utilità di un "mercato" è calmierare i prezzi dei negozi fissi del quartiere/città. Se come in questo caso i prezzi sono invece molto alti, si perde ogni significato. Qui si dovrebbe premiare la capacità/volontà di ricerca dell'acquirente, che anziché rimanere al caldo del proprio divano a fare acquisti su Amazon, vive la città e sceglie dal vivo cosa comprare, cercando occasioni. La buona volontà va premiata, non castigata all'insegna della mungitura del turista di turno. Così com'è, questo storico mercato non ha più alcun senso.

shop_address: Campo dei Mori, 3384b, 30121 Venezia VE, Italia
review_text: La galleria si trova nell'incantevole Campo dei Mori a Cannaregio e offre un'esperienza artistica intima. Lontano dalle folle, questo spazio espone arte contemporanea e opere variegate, in un dialogo affascinante la realtà del quartiere. E' il luogo ideale per scoprire nuove prospettive artistiche e godere della serenità veneziana. Un gioiello nascosto per chi cerca un'arte più ricercata, al di fuori dei circuiti tradizionali. Da vedere!

shop_address: Campo dei Mori, 3384b, 30121 Venezia VE, Italia
review_text: Nel sestiere Cannaregio lontano dalla Venezia “venduta ai turisti” c’è un angolo di autentica bellezza con atmosfera rara e suggestiva. L’artista Paolo Klavora espone le sue belle opere in questo contesto con rara gentilezza e disponibilità.. consiglio vivamente a chi voglia prendersi una boccata d’arte!!!!

shop_address: Campo dei Mori, 3384b, 30121 Venezia VE, Italia
review_text: Finalmente la Vera Venezia dell’Arte. Non una semplice galleria ma uno scrigno di emozioni. Gianpaolo una persona d’altri tempi. Atmosfera incantevole. Da vedere!!

Consiglio la visita.

review_text: I was hesitant to use this location based off some of the reviews, but gave it a go. The storage is inside a Tobacco shop.
